title: Neolloydia mandragora

intro: Neolloydia mandragora, also known as the "False Mandrake," is a species of cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its stout, branching stems and spiny appearance. The health benefits of Neolloydia mandragora include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable plant for traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Neolloydia mandragora is often prepared as a tincture or infused tea, or used in topical creams and ointments to take advantage of its wound-healing and anti-inflammatory properties.
